title: oxalis pseudohirta
intro: Oxalis pseudohirta, also known as Falsehairy Wood Sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been used to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Oxalis pseudohirta include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. Oxalis pseudohirta can be prepared for herbal use through tinctures, infusions, and salves, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions.
